description: AD lateral movement attack preconditions catalog. Covers Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting, delegation abuse, ACL abuse, DCSync, password in description, and AdminSDHolder abuse. Evidence sources: BloodHound (v4/v5), ldapdomaindump, raw LDAP, manual JSON.

AD-01.label: kerberoastable_accounts_exist
AD-01.description: Service accounts with SPNs exist that are not machine accounts, making them candidates for Kerberoasting.
AD-01.evidence_hint: BloodHound users.json: hasspn=true, enabled=true, not a computer account

AD-02.label: kerberoastable_no_aes_enforcement
AD-02.description: Kerberoastable accounts do not enforce AES-only encryption, allowing RC4 ticket requests which are faster to crack.
AD-02.evidence_hint: BloodHound users.json: hasspn=true, supportedencryptiontypes does not include AES256 only

AD-03.label: no_kerberoast_detection
AD-03.description: No indication of Kerberoast detection controls (honeypot SPNs, alerting on RC4 TGS requests).
AD-03.evidence_hint: Absence of known honeypot SPN naming conventions in user set

AD-04.label: asrep_roastable_accounts_exist
AD-04.description: User accounts exist with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled, allowing AS-REP roasting without credentials.

AD-05.label: asrep_accounts_have_privileges
AD-05.description: AS-REP roastable accounts have elevated privileges or group memberships worth targeting.
AD-05.evidence_hint: BloodHound group memberships for dontreqpreauth accounts — DA, admin groups

AD-06.label: unconstrained_delegation_non_dc
AD-06.description: Non-DC computer accounts have unconstrained delegation enabled. Any user authenticating to these systems has their TGT cached and stealable.
AD-06.evidence_hint: BloodHound computers.json: unconstraineddelegation=true, not a domain controller

AD-07.label: unconstrained_delegation_reachable
AD-07.description: Unconstrained delegation hosts are reachable/active (not stale, not disabled).

AD-08.label: constrained_delegation_protocol_transition
AD-08.description: Accounts with constrained delegation have protocol transition enabled (TrustedToAuthForDelegation), allowing S4U2Self impersonation of any user.
AD-08.evidence_hint: BloodHound users/computers: allowedtodelegate not empty, trustedtoauthfordelegation=true

AD-09.label: constrained_delegation_target_is_sensitive
AD-09.description: Constrained delegation targets include sensitive services (CIFS, LDAP, HOST on DCs or tier-0 assets).
AD-09.evidence_hint: BloodHound allowedtodelegate SPNs reference DC hostnames or sensitive services

AD-10.label: acl_genericall_on_high_value
AD-10.description: Non-admin principals have GenericAll rights over high-value targets (DA group, domain object, DC computer accounts).
AD-10.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges: GenericAll where target is high-value node

AD-11.label: acl_genericwrite_on_high_value
AD-11.description: Non-admin principals have GenericWrite over high-value targets, enabling attribute manipulation (e.g. msDS-KeyCredentialLink for shadow credentials).
AD-11.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges: GenericWrite where target is high-value node

AD-12.label: acl_writedacl_on_high_value
AD-12.description: Non-admin principals have WriteDACL over high-value targets, allowing self-granting of any permission.
AD-12.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges: WriteDacl where target is high-value node

AD-13.label: acl_writeowner_on_high_value
AD-13.description: Non-admin principals have WriteOwner over high-value targets, allowing ownership takeover and subsequent DACL modification.
AD-13.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges: WriteOwner where target is high-value node

AD-14.label: acl_forcechangepassword_on_privileged
AD-14.description: Non-admin principals have ForceChangePassword rights over privileged accounts.
AD-14.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges: ForceChangePassword where target is admin/DA member

AD-15.label: dcsync_rights_outside_da
AD-15.description: Accounts outside Domain Admins/Domain Controllers hold both Replicating Directory Changes and Replicating Directory Changes All rights on the domain object, enabling DCSync.
AD-15.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges: GetChanges + GetChangesAll on domain node from non-DA principals

AD-16.label: dcsync_account_is_enabled
AD-16.description: The account holding DCSync rights is enabled and not a legitimate sync account.
AD-16.evidence_hint: BloodHound users.json: enabled=true for accounts with GetChanges+GetChangesAll

AD-17.label: password_in_description
AD-17.description: User or computer accounts have plaintext credentials or password hints stored in the description field.
AD-17.evidence_hint: BloodHound/ldapdomaindump: description field contains password-related keywords

AD-18.label: password_in_description_account_enabled
AD-18.description: Accounts with passwords in descriptions are enabled and have not been rotated.
AD-18.evidence_hint: BloodHound users.json: enabled=true, pwdlastset old, description contains credential

AD-19.label: adminsdholder_unexpected_principals
AD-19.description: Unexpected non-admin principals have permissions on the AdminSDHolder object, which propagates to all protected accounts via SDProp.
AD-19.evidence_hint: BloodHound ACL edges on AdminSDHolder node from non-standard principals

AD-20.label: adminsdholder_propagation_active
AD-20.description: SDProp is running (default: every 60 min), meaning AdminSDHolder ACL changes will propagate to protected groups.
AD-20.evidence_hint: Absence of evidence it is disabled; assumed active unless explicitly blocked

AD-21.label: stale_privileged_accounts
AD-21.description: Privileged accounts (DA, EA, BA members) have not logged in recently but remain enabled — lower detection risk when abused.
AD-21.evidence_hint: BloodHound users.json: group member of high-value group, lastlogontimestamp > 90 days, enabled=true

AD-22.label: no_privileged_account_tiering
AD-22.description: Privileged accounts are used on non-tier-0 systems (no tiering model enforced), increasing exposure.
AD-22.evidence_hint: BloodHound: DA/EA accounts have sessions on workstations or member servers

AD-23.label: spn_on_da_account
AD-23.description: A Domain Admin account has an SPN set, making it directly Kerberoastable with immediate DA-level impact.
AD-23.evidence_hint: BloodHound: hasspn=true on account that is member of Domain Admins

AD-24.label: weak_password_policy
AD-24.description: Domain password policy allows short or non-complex passwords, increasing likelihood of offline crack success.
AD-24.evidence_hint: ldapdomaindump domain_policy.json: minPwdLength < 12, complexity not enforced

AD-25.label: no_laps_on_workstations
AD-25.description: Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) not deployed on workstations, meaning shared local admin passwords enable lateral movement.
AD-25.evidence_hint: BloodHound computers.json: haslaps=false on workstation-class machines

ad_kerberoast_v1.description: Offline password cracking via Kerberoasting. Request TGS tickets for SPN-linked accounts, crack RC4-encrypted tickets offline.

ad_kerberoast_da_v1.description: Kerberoasting targeting a Domain Admin account directly. Highest-impact variant.

ad_asrep_roast_v1.description: Offline password cracking via AS-REP roasting. No credentials required. Targets accounts with pre-auth disabled.

ad_unconstrained_delegation_v1.description: TGT theft via unconstrained delegation. Coerce authentication from high-value accounts to delegation host, extract cached TGTs.

ad_constrained_delegation_s4u_v1.description: Privilege escalation via S4U2Self/S4U2Proxy abuse on accounts with protocol transition enabled.

ad_acl_abuse_v1.description: Privilege escalation via abusable ACL edges (GenericAll, GenericWrite, WriteDACL, WriteOwner) on high-value AD objects.

ad_acl_forcechangepw_v1.description: Account takeover via ForceChangePassword ACL edge on privileged accounts.

ad_dcsync_v1.description: Credential dumping via DCSync. Non-DC account replicates domain secrets including krbtgt hash.

ad_password_in_description_v1.description: Credential harvesting from AD description fields. Plaintext passwords stored as admin convenience.

ad_adminsdholder_v1.description: Persistence via AdminSDHolder ACL abuse. Grants persistent access to all protected accounts via SDProp propagation.

ad_laps_absent_v1.description: Lateral movement via shared local admin credentials. No LAPS means cracking one machine's local admin unlocks many.
